title: Call a VPC Module
description: Write a root Terraform configuration that calls a local VPC module, passes variables, and uses the module's output.
starterCode:
# Write a root Terraform configuration that:
# 1. Calls a local module at source = "./modules/vpc"
# 2. Passes: cidr_block = "10.0.0.0/16" and environment = "production"
# 3. Creates an aws_subnet using module.vpc.vpc_id
#    cidr_block = "10.0.1.0/24", availability_zone = "us-east-1a"
# 4. Outputs 'vpc_id' using the module output

# Module call

# Subnet resource

# Output
hints: Module blocks: module "name" { source = "path", variable = value }
After adding a module call, run terraform init to initialize it
Local module sources start with ./ or ../

validationRules.errorMessage: Add a module block calling the vpc module
Set source to the local module path ./modules/vpc
Add an output for the vpc_id

simulatedOutput.success:
Initializing modules...
- vpc in ./modules/vpc

Terraform will perform the following actions:
  + module.vpc.aws_vpc.this will be created
  + aws_subnet.app will be created

Plan: 2 to add, 0 to change, 0 to destroy.

Outputs:
  vpc_id = (known after apply)
simulatedOutput.failure: Module configuration error. Check module block syntax, source path, module output reference, and subnet resource.
